commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

commit.message:
feat(agents): Level 3 Phase 5 — dynamic few-shot retrieval + Vertex AI embeddings + pgvector

Dual-mode similarity search for injecting past successful queries as few-shot examples:
- Mode A (pgvector): Vertex AI text-multilingual-embedding-002 (768d) + pgvector cosine
  distance for semantic similarity. Embeddings computed fire-and-forget in _log_query().
- Mode B (TF-IDF): IntentClassifier's TF-IDF cosine in Python (~1ms, zero API cost).
  Used as fallback during cold start or when Vertex AI unavailable.

New files:
- few_shot_retriever.py: dual-mode retriever (pgvector -> TF-IDF fallback)
- embedding_service.py: Vertex AI embedding with Redis cache (1h TTL)
- migration 174: embedding vector(768) column + IVFFlat index on agent_query_logs

Integration:
- base_analyst_service: few-shot retrieval before prompt building + embedding in _log_query
- nlp_preprocessor: build_enriched_prompt accepts few_shot_section parameter
- Cold start guard: < 10 successful logs = no few-shot (current behavior unchanged)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Switch default model to BGE-base (EmbeddingGemma external weights broken)

EmbeddingGemma ONNX loading via commit_from_memory doesn't correctly
resolve external weights (model.onnx_data), producing garbage
embeddings with zero vector similarity. Switching default to
BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 (768 dims, proven fastembed support).

Model change detection will auto-trigger clean reindex on deploy.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Limit escapeHTML to simple value access patterns only

Instead of listing individual HTML-safe helpers (render, content_for,
yield, etc.), only apply escapeHTML to expressions that clearly represent
user data: ivars, lvars, and zero-arg attribute calls (object.name).
Everything else (helper calls, blocks, yield) uses String() which is
stripped in template literals. This correctly handles all HTML-returning
helpers without needing to enumerate them.

commit.message:
S9: move to non-default — FREEZE_PAGE replay doesn't need cleanup locks

FREEZE_PAGE replay uses XLogReadBufferForRedo (regular exclusive content
lock), NOT LockBufferForCleanup. Buffer pins from concurrent scanners
do not block it. Only HEAP2_PRUNE and BTREE_VACUUM records need cleanup
locks, but hot_standby_feedback=on prevents their generation (standby
xmins prevent dead-tuple removal on the primary).

Default suite is now: S1 S2 S3 S4 S7 S8 S10 S11 S12 S14.

fix: delete freeReport.tsx (SDK version shadowing .ts) + stagger drip emails

- freeReport.tsx used old Resend SDK and had no logging; TypeScript was
  resolving .tsx before .ts so the new raw-fetch freeReport.ts was never
  called — meaning the free report email silently failed every time
- drip.ts: replace Promise.all with sequential sends + 700ms gaps to
  stay under Resend's 2 req/sec rate limit (was causing 2/3 drip emails
  to fail with 429 on every scan)

Fix build: remove unused scanningProjectId state variable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix scoring: per-factor energy filtering instead of blanket gate

The blanket isEnergyRelevant gate dropped all items whose RSS
descriptions lacked energy keywords, zeroing out most categories.

Now each factor applies its own relevance logic:
- Nation-state: always scores (indicators ARE energy-targeting APTs)
- KEV: only energy-relevant vendors/descriptions (via isEnergyRelevant)
- ICS/SCADA: always scores (ICS content IS energy by definition)
- Vendor alerts: only if flagged isEnergyRelevant

Last-24h counts follow the same pattern: nation-state and ICS count
from all items, KEV and total count energy-flagged only.

Agent names like "sunday" were being passed to requested_by_user_id
(a UUID column) in job creation, causing Prisma UUID parse errors.
Removed the field from agent-initiated jobs since it's optional.

commit.message:
Bump version to 0.4.0: Add custom profile photo upload

Users can upload a profile photo (JPG/PNG/WebP, max 5MB) from the
Profile tab. The photo is stored as a media library attachment and
replaces Gravatar in the account header. Uploading previews and
saves instantly without a page reload. Old photos are deleted when
a new one is uploaded.

commit.message:
Initial commit: FormCraft — full-stack form builder SaaS

Complete MVP with:
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Prisma + SQLite database (User, Form, Response, Payment models)
- NextAuth credentials authentication (register, login, sessions)
- Drag-and-drop form builder with 14 field types
- Public form rendering with themed styling and validation
- Stripe integration (LTD + subscription checkout, webhooks)
- Response viewer with table, detail panel, and CSV export
- Dashboard with search, form management, user menu
- Account page with plan display and upgrade flow
- Share/embed modal with direct link and iframe code
- Landing page with hero, features, pricing, testimonials
- Plan-based access control (free: 3 forms/100 responses)
- Auto-save with debounced API calls
- 404, checkout success/cancel pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rework to tabbed layout: Rules, Register, Teams Portal, Challenges & Tools, Help

- Full tabbed interface replacing single-page scroll layout
- Tab 1 (Rules): rule cards, key dates table, scoring rubric
- Tab 2 (Register): inline team/free-agent option cards, opens modal on click
- Tab 3 (Registration Portal): live team grid from Airtable, click to view detail
  - TeamDetailModal shows members, use case, grayed-out "Coming Soon" workspace btn
  - After registration, auto-redirects to Teams tab
- Tab 4 (Challenges & Tools): callout explaining own problem statements welcome,
  judging criteria (relevance, business impact, AI integration), problem cards, tools
- Tab 5 (Help): FAQ accordion, contacts, mentor program
- Fixed-height portal layout (flex column, overflow-y on body)
- Top bar: gradient header with brand + countdown + register CTA
- Phase strip: compact 4-step timeline below header

Fix admin feedback API authorization and authentication

🔧 API Authorization Fix:
- Replace hardcoded email check with session-based admin detection
- Add missing authOptions import to getServerSession call
- Enable proper admin feedback management functionality

📝 Technical Details:
- Updated PATCH method in /api/admin/feedback route
- Changed from `session.user.email === 'gdogra@gmail.com'` to `session.user.isAdmin === true`
- Fixed getServerSession() to use authOptions parameter
- Resolves 404 errors when updating feedback status or sending admin responses

✅ Production Ready:
- Admin dashboard feedback management now functional
- Proper session-based authorization implemented
- API endpoints working with current authentication flow

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Setup wizard UX + deletion log batching (#293)

## Summary
- Add interactive in-Discord setup wizard with defaults/customize paths
— users pick a mod role, then choose between auto-creating all channels
or walking through each setting one-by-one, with a confirmation step
before executing
- Consolidate `/setup` and `/setup-all` into a single `/setup` command
- Centralize bot permissions and invite URL into a shared module,
replacing Administrator with exact required permissions
- Batch uncached deletion log entries to reduce noise from mass-deletes
of old messages

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: set --min-replicas 1 to prevent ACA scaling to zero

Azure Container Apps scales to zero when there's no HTTP traffic,
which kills background daemon tasks mid-execution. Both the manual
deploy script and GitHub Actions workflow now enforce min-replicas 1.

commit.message:
Activate 5 high-value state golf associations (FL, AZ, NJ, GAP, NCGA)

Investigation found all 5 "inactive" associations actually use Golf Genius
— GG links were on sub-pages or results pages, not the main calendar.

Activated:
- FSGA (Florida) — GG Hub discovery, live scoring + results
- AGA (Arizona) — GG Hub + pages, also uses BlueGolf for calendar
- NJSGA (New Jersey) — rebranded to njgolf.org, results link to GG pages
- GAP (Philadelphia, PA/NJ/DE) — results link to GG scoring portal
- NCGA (Northern California) — BlueGolf schedule + GG leaderboards

State coverage: 36 active associations covering 38 states (up from 31/35).
Remaining inactive: 15 orgs covering 13 states (mostly small/rural).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: golf-tracker
repository.description: Golf tournament tracker for local news research - tracks PGA Tour and other professional golf leagues with high school/college biographical data

commit.message:
feat: onboarding v3 — real consulting conversation + timeout fixes

Technical fixes:
- Gunicorn timeout: 120s → 300s (render.yaml)
- Anthropic client timeout: 25s → 300s
- Per-task timeouts: research 40s → 240s, assessment/action_plan → 120s
- Frontend onboarding timeout: 90s → 300s, safety timer → 360s
- Session persistence: try/finally wrapper on process_turn for crash recovery

Conversation redesign:
- Smart basic_info extraction: LLM extracts ALL fields per message, asks only what's missing
- Inline dynamic questions: LLM decides follow_up vs move_on per turn (no pre-generation step)
- Depth-based topic progression: move on at 3+ data points, not fixed question count
- Critical rules injected into every persona prompt (no repeating questions, specific not generic)

Closing stage upgrades:
- Summary: must reference specific client data (names, numbers, quotes)
- Research: uses business_type for industry-specific benchmarks, direct number comparison
- Assessment: forbidden from generic advice, must cite specific numbers
- Action Plan: missions must be specific (verb + target + metric), uses client's challenge/goal

Tests: 244 passing (242 existing + 2 new E2E with Sproutix client story)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
